key_insight: The paper closes a long-standing gap in one-dimensional derivative-free stochastic convex optimization by achieving the optimal O(1/\u221aT) rate with noisy function evaluations.
engineering_relevance: This gives a sharp benchmark for black-box optimization in settings where gradients are unavailable, such as simulation-driven tuning and noisy experimental control.
method: Designs a computationally efficient zero-order algorithm for convex minimization on [0,1].
Uses only noisy function evaluations from a subGaussian oracle.
Achieves the minimax-optimal O(1/\u221aT) convergence rate, matching the lower bound.
evidence_signal: The abstract states the method matches the known \u03a9(1/\u221aT) lower bound, closing the previous logarithmic gap in one dimension.
watch_item: The guarantee is proved for a one-dimensional convex function on [0,1] with subGaussian noise, so extension to higher dimensions or different noise models is not covered.
paper_title: Sharp Optimal Algorithm for Derivative-Free Stochastic Convex Optimization in One Dimension
